question: Based on Figure 1.2-2 (functional boundaries) and Figure 5.1-3 (RCS schematic), are the NSSS boundaries appropriately defined for safety classification?

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends provided for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0651.title: DSRS 15.0, Rev 0: Introduction - Transient and Accident AnalysesI.8.A Identification of Causes and Frequency Classification
nuscale_ch01_0651.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for transient and accident analyses, noting areas of full and partial compliance with DSRS 15.0 requirements. While most criteria are met, some aspects—such as frequency analysis for AOOs and specific acceptance criteria for fuel cladding and LOCA—only partially conform to guidance. The identification of causes and frequency classification is fully addressed, with exceptions mainly related to COL applicant requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0360.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.4 Components of the RCPB, quality classification and seismic design classification
nuscale_ch01_0360.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) and related systems. It states that the CVCS is located outside the Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) and generally conforms to applicable standards, except for certain criteria regarding holdup tanks, which are not relevant to the NuScale design. Other referenced standards either fully conform or are not applicable to the NuScale reactor type.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. The RCS removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining the integrity of the pressure boundary to prevent radionuclide release. It interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control systems.

nuscale_ch01_0032.title: Radioactive Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0032.summary: The section describes the radioactive waste management system at the NuScale Power Plant, noting that liquid, gaseous, and solid waste systems are detailed in Chapter 11, along with effluent monitoring. It also outlines the general arrangement of major structures, focusing on the Reactor Building (RXB), which is a reinforced, seismically-rated concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the nuclear power modules (NPMs) in a central reactor pool.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the centrally located Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references figures for detailed layouts and notes that some buildings, like the administration and training building, are outside the report's scope.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant's certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified plant portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0727.title: DSRS 15.2.8, Rev 0: Feedwater System Pipe Break Inside and Outside Containment (PWR)II.3 Calculated Site Boundary Doses
nuscale_ch01_0727.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for feedwater system pipe breaks inside and outside containment, specifically regarding calculated site boundary doses. It states that the analysis conforms to the applicable requirements, with no exceptions noted for this criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0477.title: DSRS 11.3, Rev 0: Gaseous Waste Management SystemII.3 Seismic Design and Quality Group Classification of Components and Structures Housing Gaseous Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0477.summary: This section confirms that the seismic design and quality group classification of components and structures housing the Gaseous Waste Management System fully conforms to regulatory requirements. No exceptions or additional notes are identified for this criterion.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of reactor coolant pressure boundary components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, all designed to meet regulatory requirements and maintain pressure containment.

nuscale_ch01_0160.title: DSRS 3.11, Rev. 0: Environmental Qualification of Mechanical and Electrical EquipmentII.25 Design and Procurement Specifications
nuscale_ch01_0160.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to the environmental qualification of mechanical and electrical equipment, as well as design and procurement specifications. It notes that certain requirements, such as DSRS 3.11 II.25, are not applicable to NuScale and are instead COL applicant items, while other standards (e.g., SRP 3.12 and 3.13) are met with no exceptions. Additionally, guidance specific to BWR plants is marked as not applicable to NuScale’s design.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It confirms that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III standards, with appropriate quality classifications, and that the 2013 Edition of the ASME BPVC is used as the code of record for the design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0353.title: SRP 9.3.2, Rev 3: Process and Post-Accident Sampling SystemsII.4 Seismic Design and Quality Group Classification
nuscale_ch01_0353.summary: The section states that the NuScale design fully conforms to the seismic design and quality group classification requirements for process and post-accident sampling systems as outlined in SRP 9.3.2, Rev 3. There are no exceptions or additional notes for this criterion, indicating full compliance.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It employs passive safety systems—including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems—that do not rely on electrical power, and its steel containment is partially immersed in water to provide an effective passive heat sink. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering via turbine bypass, control rods, and soluble boron adjustments.

nuscale_ch01_0817.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.11 D&S System extremely high probability of functioning in the event of an AOO.
nuscale_ch01_0817.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with DSRS 15.9.A requirements for thermal hydraulic stability. The NuScale design does not use detect and suppress (D&S) methods or exclusion zones; instead, existing reactor trip signals act preemptively to prevent conditions that could lead to flow instabilities, making additional D&S measures unnecessary. The system partially conforms to some criteria by ensuring reactor trips occur before safety limits are challenged, but does not employ or require backup D&S options or related instrumentation.

nuscale_ch01_0118.title: 1.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
nuscale_ch01_0118.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant's certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0559.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.10 RG 8.19
nuscale_ch01_0559.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details which regulatory guides are fully conformed to, partially conformed to, or not applicable, with specific references to supporting sections in the safety analysis report. The table provides a structured overview of compliance status for each guide relevant to radiation protection.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The section outlines the principal design criteria for the NuScale plant, focusing on the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) and the overall facility process. It describes the reactor core’s heat transfer process, natural circulation of coolant, steam generation, and the flow of superheated steam to the turbine and condenser, emphasizing site-specific details for cooling systems and equipment layout.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed drawings that define system boundaries, which can be cross-referenced with Section B's general plant descriptions to determine if the NSSS boundaries for safety classification are appropriately delineated.

Section A provides detailed drawings and schematics (including functional boundaries) that visually define the NSSS boundaries, which are necessary to assess whether these boundaries meet the safety classification requirements outlined in Section B's regulatory criteria for transient and accident analyses.

Section A provides detailed drawings, including functional boundaries in Figure 1.2-2, which visually clarify and specify the system boundaries that are described in general terms in Section B's system description and schematic (Figure 5.1-3), thus connecting the visual and textual definitions of the NSSS boundaries for safety classification.

Section A provides detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify system boundaries, while Section B presents the general arrangement and functional boundaries of major structures, so together they define and contextualize the NSSS boundaries relevant for safety classification.
